{{Short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
| Badge                 = Flag of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines.svg
| Nickname              = ''Vincy Heat''
| Association           = [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Football Federation]] (SVGFF)
| Confederation         = [[CONCACAF]] (North America)
| Sub-confederation     = [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] (Caribbean)
| Coach                 = [[Ezra Hendrickson]]
| Captain               = [[Cornelius Stewart]]
| Most caps             = [[Cornelius Stewart]] (79)
| Top scorer            = [[Shandel Samuel]] (32)
| Home Stadium          = [[Arnos Vale Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = VIN
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|VIN}}
| FIFA max              = 73
| FIFA max date         = October 2007
| FIFA min              = 180
| FIFA min date         = January 2017, December 2018 – February 2019
| Elo Rank              = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines}}
| Elo max               = 104
| Elo max date          = 14 January 2007
| Elo min               = 182
| Elo min date          = November 2018
| pattern_la1           = 
| pattern_b1            = _SVN_h22
| pattern_ra1           = 
| pattern_sh1           = _shorts
| pattern_so1           = _socks
| leftarm1              = FFC90E
| body1                 = FFC90E
| rightarm1             = FFC90E
| shorts1               = FFC90E
| socks1                = FFC90E
| pattern_la2           = 
| pattern_b2            = _SVN_a22
| pattern_ra2           = 
| pattern_sh2           = _shorts
| pattern_so2           = _socks
| leftarm2              = 0000B8
| body2                 = 0000B8
| rightarm2             = 0000B8
| shorts2               = 0000B8
| socks2                = 0000B8
| First game            = {{nowrap|{{fb|VIN|colonial}} 1–1 {{fb-rt|BRB|1885}}}}<br>([[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]]; 12 September 1936)
| Largest win           = {{nowrap|{{fb|MSR}} 0–11 {{fb-rt|VIN}}}}<br>([[Woodlands, Montserrat|Woodlands]], [[Montserrat]]; 7 May 1995)
| Largest loss          = {{fb|MEX}} 11–0 {{fb-rt|VIN}}<br>([[Mexico City]], Mexico; 6 December 1992)
| Regional name         = [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]]
| Regional cup apps     = 1
| Regional cup first    = [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]]
| Regional cup best     = Group stage ([[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]])
| medaltemplates        = 
{{MedalCompetition|[[CFU Championship]] / [[Caribbean Cup]]}}
{{MedalSilver|[[1979 CFU Championship|1979 Suriname]]|Team}}
{{MedalSilver|[[1981 CFU Championship|1981 Puerto Rico]]|Team}}
{{MedalSilver|[[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995 Cayman Islands and Jamaica]]|Team}}
}}

The '''Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team''', recognised as '''St Vincent and the Grenadines''' by [[FIFA]],<ref>{{cite web | url=https://inside.fifa.com/fifa-world-ranking/VIN?gender=men | title=Inside FIFA }}</ref> represents [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]] in men's international [[Association football|football]]. It is controlled by the [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines Football Federation]].

The team finished second in the [[Caribbean Cup]] in 1995, and participated at the [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup]]. Its first [[FIFA World Cup qualification]] attempt was for the 1994 edition, and it has entered every World Cup qualification since.

==History==

===1936–1995===
Saint Vincent and the Grenadines played their first two international matches, against their neighbour [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] in 1936 - both matches ending in a 1–1 draw. In the '60s and '70s, they played several editions of the [[Windward Islands Tournament]], winning the 1965 and 1966 competitions. They also participated in the [[1979 CFU Championship|1979]], qualifying to the final stage and finishing in second place behind [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]]. In the following edition, in [[1981 CFU Championship|1981]], they reached the final stage but again finished in second, this time two points off of [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]].

Saint Vincent and the Grenadines qualified for the inaugural [[1989 Caribbean Cup]], finishing top of their qualification group including a 9–0 victory over [[Sint Maarten national football team|Sint Maarten]]. In the finals, they would fail to get out of the group, finishing bottom behind [[Grenada national football team|Grenada]] and [[Netherlands Antilles national football team|Netherlands Antilles]]. They would reach the final phase of the Caribbean Cup again in consecutive years, [[1992 Caribbean Cup|1992]] and [[1993 Caribbean Cup|1993]], without much luck, finishing last and second last respectively.

At the end of 1992, the ''Vincy Heat'' participated in their first [[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|World Cup qualifiers]]. They got through the first rounds, eliminating [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]] (3–2 on aggregate) and [[Suriname national football team|Suriname]] (2–1 on aggregate), to advance to the second phase, where they finished bottom of the group, with six losses in as many games. Especially painful was the 11–0 defeat that [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]] dealt them at the [[Azteca Stadium]], the worst in their history.

===1996 Gold Cup===
Saint Vincent and the Grenadines qualified for the [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup]], by obtaining the runner-up position in the [[1995 Caribbean Cup]]. During qualification for the Caribbean Cup, ''Vincy Heat'' had dispatched [[Montserrat national football team|Montserrat]] 20–0 over two legs. They had gone on to finish top of their group in the main competition and defeated [[Cuba national football team|Cuba]] in the semi-finals 3–2, however [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]] proved too strong in the final and won 5–0. In the 1996 Gold Cup, the ''Vincy Heat'' failed to register a victory, losing 5–0 to [[Mexico national football team|Mexico]] and 3–0 to [[Guatemala national football team|Guatemala]].

===1996–present=== 
Saint Vincent and the Grenadines would qualify again for the [[1996 Caribbean Cup]] but this time finish bottom of their group. It would be eleven years before they qualified again, on goals scored, following their 8–0 thrashing of [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]] with five goals from [[Shandel Samuel]] which saw them edge out [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]]. In the [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007 tournament]], ''Vincy Heat'' only managed one win, against [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] 2–0, and finished bottom of their group again.

In [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1998 World Cup qualifiers]], the Saint Vincentian team beat [[Puerto Rico national football team|Puerto Rico]] 9–1 over two legs, then defeated [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|Saint Kitts and Nevis]] on away goals, thanks to a 2–2 draw in [[Basseterre]]. In the third round of qualifying they finished with six defeats in six games, including an 11–3 loss to [[Honduras national football team|Honduras]]. In the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2002 qualifiers]], they beat the [[United States Virgin Islands national football team|U.S. Virgin Islands]] 14–1 on aggregate, [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|Saint Kitts and Nevis]] (4-2) and [[Antigua and Barbuda national football team|Antigua and Barbuda]] (5–2) but again finished without a win in the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF semi-finals|qualification semi-finals]].

They improved in the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2006 qualifiers]] beating [[Nicaragua national football team|Nicaragua]] to advance to the third round for the third consecutive time, and obtaining two victories over [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|Saint Kitts and Nevis]], finishing in third place. However, in the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2010 qualifiers]], they were eliminated for the first time in the second qualifying round, at the hands of [[Canada men's national soccer team|Canada]], who prevailed with an aggregate score of 7–1. In [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2014 qualification]], Saint Vincent and the Grenadines won once against [[Grenada national football team|Grenada]] to finish 13 points behind [[Guatemala national football team|Guatemala]]. In [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2018 World Cup qualification]], they managed to beat [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] 6–6 on away goals, then beat [[Aruba national football team|Aruba]] 3–2 to make it to the fourth round group stage. There they failed to get a single point, conceding 34 goals and finishing bottom of their group, including a 9–3 loss to [[Guatemala national football team|Guatemala]].

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 29 May
|time       = 
|team1      = {{fb-rt|VIN}}
|score      = 1–1
|team2      = {{fb|BVI}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
*[[Kirtney Franklyn]] {{goal|84}}
|goals2     =
*[[Jerry Wiltshire]] {{goal|55}}
|location   = [[Arnos Vale, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines|Arnos Vale]], Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
|stadium    = [[Arnos Vale Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = D
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 4 June
|time       = 
|team1      = {{fb-rt|VIN}}
|score      = 6–0 
|team2      = {{fb|AIA}}
|report     =
|goals1     = 
*[[Oalex Anderson|Anderson]] {{goal|20||69}}
*[[Cornelius Stewart|Stewart]] {{goal|25|pen.}}
*[[Micah Joseph|Joseph]] {{goal|59}}
*[[Kyle Edwards (footballer, born 1997)|Edwards]] {{goal|61}}
*[[Kirtney Franklyn|Franklyn]] {{goal|85}}
|goals2     =
|location   = [[Arnos Vale, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines|Arnos Vale]], Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
|stadium    = [[Arnos Vale Stadium]]
|attendance = 3,500
|referee    = Steffon Dewar ([[Jamaica Football Federation|Jamaica]])
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 June
|time       = 
|team1      = {{fb-rt|PUR}}
|score      = 2–1
|team2      = {{fb|VIN|wrap=yes}}
|report     =
|goals1     =
*[[Leandro Antonetti|Antonetti]] {{goal|11}}
*[[Steven Echevarria|Echevarria]] {{goal|77}}
|goals2     =
*[[Oalex Anderson|Anderson]] {{goal|47}}
|location   = [[Mayagüez, Puerto Rico|Mayagüez]], Puerto Rico
|stadium    = [[Mayagüez Athletics Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Randy Solano ([[Dominican Football Federation|Dominican Republic]])
|result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 5 October
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|VIN}}
|score      = 7–0
|team2      = {{fb|CUB}} (domestic team)
|report     = https://www.beinsports.com/en-us/soccer/friendlies/st-vincent--grenadines-vs-cuba-2025-10-05#OverView
|goals1     =
*[[Oalex Anderson|O. Anderson]] {{goal|14}}
*[[Steven Pierre|S. Pierre]] {{goal|29}}
*[[Cornelius Stewart|C. Stewart]] {{goal|44}}
*[[Garret Leigertwood|G. Leigertwood]] {{goal|74||90+1}}
*[[Azinho Solomon|A. Solomon]] {{goal|89}}
*[[Malcolm Stewart (footballer)|M. Stewart]] {{goal|90+4}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Arnos Vale Stadium]]
|location   = [[Arnos Vale, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines|Arnos Vale]], Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
|attendance =
|referee    =
|result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 8 October
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|VIN}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|CUB}} (domestic team)
|report     = https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c20vden9zg3t
|goals1     =
*[[Oalex Anderson|O. Anderson]] {{goal|75}}
|goals2     =
|location   = [[Arnos Vale, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines|Arnos Vale]], Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
|stadium    = [[Arnos Vale Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 12 November
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|DOM}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|VIN|wrap=yes}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
*[[Heinz Mörschel|Mörschel]] {{goal|17}}
*[[Erick Paniagua|Paniagua]] {{goal|57}}
|goals2     =
|location   = [[Santiago de los caballeros|Santiago]], Dominican Republic 
|stadium    = [[Estadio Cibao FC]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 15 November
|time       = {{UTZ|20:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|VIN}}
|score      = 3–1
|team2      = {{fb|LCA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
*[[Oalex Anderson|Anderson]] {{goal|12}}
*[[Diel Spring|Spring]] {{goal|13}}
*[[Azinho Solomon|Solomon]] {{goal|62}}
|goals2     =
*[[Caniggia Elva|Elva]] {{goal|45}}
|location   = [[Santiago de los caballeros|Santiago]], Dominican Republic 
|stadium    = [[Estadio Cibao FC]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = W
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|round= [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date= 26 March
|time= {{UTZ|20:30|-4}}
|team1= {{fb-rt|BOE}}
|score= 3–1
|team2= {{fb|VIN|wrap=yes}}
|report= https://www.concacaf.com/concacaf-series/news/anguilla-bonaire-saint-martin-pick-up-wins-in-concacaf-series/
|goals1= *[[Devin Koorn|Koorn]] {{goal|39}}
*[[Lemus Christopher|Christopher]] {{goal|57|o.g.}}
*[[Christopher Isenia|Isenia]] {{goal|77|pen.}}
|goals2= [[Steven Pierre|Pierre]] {{goal|37}}
|location= [[Rincon, Bonaire|Rincon]], Bonaire
|stadium= [[Stadion Antonio Trenidat]]
|attendance= 
|referee= Norberto da Silva ([[Curaçao Football Federation|Curaçao]])
|result= L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2025–26 CONCACAF Series]]
|date       = 29 March
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|BRB}}
|score      = 2–3
|team2      = {{fb|VIN|wrap=yes}}
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-29/barbados-vs-st-vincent-and-the-grenadines/4126783
|goals1     = *[[Ajani Banton|Banton]] {{goal|51}}
*[[Jaron Oughterson|Oughterson]] {{goal|59}}
|goals2     = *[[Kirtney Franklyn|Franklyn]] {{goal|28}}
*[[Azinho Solomon|Solomon]] {{goal|38}}
*[[Oalex Anderson|Anderson]] {{goal|78}}
|location   = [[Rincon, Bonaire|Rincon]], Bonaire
|stadium    = [[Stadion Antonio Trenidat]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = [[Evens Julmis]] ([[Bahamas Football Association|Bahamas]])
|result     = W
}}

==Coaching staff==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
!style="color:#0000B8;background:#FFC90E|Position
!style="color:#0000B8;background:#FFC90E|Name
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Ezra Hendrickson]]
|-
|Assistant coaches
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Rohan Webster<br>{{flagicon|VIN}} Otis Devine
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Kemar Henry
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Oswald Montclair
|-
|Massage therapist
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Denzil Appleton
|-
|Team doctors
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Dr. Andriw Cooper<br>{{flagicon|VIN}} Dr. Katherine Austin
|-
|Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Marlon Gavins<br>{{flagicon|VIN}} Waylon Foster<br>{{flagicon|VIN}} Priscilla Irwin<br>{{flagicon|VIN}} Jebediah Andrews
|-
|Match analyst
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Denny Vincent
|-
|Performance analyst
|{{flagicon|VIN}} Windell Houston
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Kendale Mercury]]
|-
|}

===Coaching history===
[[File:Zoran Vranes interview 2014.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Zoran Vraneš]] became the manager of St. Vincent and the Grenadines in 2004]]
{{div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Jochen Figge]] (1985)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Elliot Millington]] (1990–1992)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Jorge Ramos (football coach)|Jorge Ramos]] (1992)
*{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Ignacio Vergelli]] (1992–1995)
*{{flagicon|JAM}} [[Lenny Taylor (footballer)|Lenny Taylor]] (1995–1996)
*{{flagicon|TRI}} [[Bertille St. Clair]] (1996)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Samuel Carrington]] (1996–2000)
*{{flagicon|JAM}} [[Lenny Taylor (footballer)|Lenny Taylor]] (2000–2001)
*{{flagicon|JAM}} [[Elvis Brown]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Adrian Shaw (footballer)|Adrian Shaw]] (2003)
*{{flagicon|SCG}}/{{flagicon|SER|2004}} [[Zoran Vraneš]] (2004–2007)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} Roger Gurley (2008)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Kendale Mercury]] (2009)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Stewart Hall (football coach)|Stewart Hall]] (2009–2011)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Colwyn Rowe]] (2011)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Cornelius Huggins]] (2012–2016)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Keith Ollivierre]] (2016)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Cornelius Huggins]] (2016–2018)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Keith Ollivierre]] (2018)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Kendale Mercury]] (2018–2023)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Bishon Williams]] (2023)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Theon Gordon]] (2023–2024)
*{{flagicon|VIN}} [[Ezra Hendrickson]] (2024–Present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] against [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|Saint Kitts and Nevis]] on 20 May 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Here's your Vincy Heat starting XI for tonight's international friendly vs St Kitts & Nevis! |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo?fbid=1229785512482689&set=a.620485316746048 |website=Facebook |publisher=SVG Football Federation |access-date=21 May 2025 |date=20 May 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 26 March 2025, after the match against [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Kenyan Lynch]]|age={{birth date and age|1985|12|15|df=yes}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Garwin Davis]]|age={{bda|2004|4|7|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[System 3 FC|System 3]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Samir Bristol]]|age=|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Fitz Hughes Predators|North Leeward Predators]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jamal Yorke]]|age={{bda|df=y|1991|10|9}}|caps=33|goals=0|club=[[Parham F.C.|Parham]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Najima Burgin]]|age={{bda|1996|10|16|df=y}}|caps=22|goals=0|club=[[Jebelle FC|Jebelle]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Nigel Charles]]|age={{bda|2003|3|20|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Camdonia Chelsea SC|Camdonia Chelsea]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Dimitri Thomas]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|03|01|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Jebelle FC|Jebelle]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Nazir McBurnette]]|age={{bda|1993|2|18|df=y}}|caps=58|goals=5|club=[[Hope International FC|Hope International]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Diel Spring]]|age={{bda|2000|12|26|df=y}}|caps=34|goals=5|club=[[Fitz Hughes Predators|North Leeward Predators]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kennijha Joseph]]|age={{bda|2000|8|10|df=y}}|caps=23|goals=0|club=[[Layou FC|Layou]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Addeen Charles]]|age={{bda|1996|3|17|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Fitz Hughes Predators|North Leeward Predators]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kirtney Franklyn]]|age={{bda|df=y|2005|3|5}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Fitz Hughes Predators|North Leeward Predators]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Bishon Richards]]|age={{bda|2003|6|1|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[Hope International FC|Hope International]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Saviola Blake]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|04|04|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=''Unknown''}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Cornelius Stewart]]|age={{bda|df=y|1989|10|07}}|caps=76|goals=25|club=[[PS Barito Putera|Barito Putera]]|clubnat=IDN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Azinho Solomon]]|age={{bda|1994|10|12|df=y}}|caps=47|goals=7|club=[[System 3 FC|System 3]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Steven Pierre]]|age={{bda|df=y|2005|5|21}}|caps=17|goals=4|club=[[Jebelle FC|Jebelle]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Garret Leigertwood]]|age={{bda|df=y|2000|2|19}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Avenues United FC|Avenues United]]|clubnat=SVG}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Lemus Christopher]]|age={{bda|1995|5|27|df=y}}|caps=21|goals=0|club=[[CS Longueuil]]|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Dondre Abraham]]|age={{bda|2006|5|24|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Concord Rangers F.C.|Concord Rangers]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Josh Stowe]]|age={{bda|2003|5|6|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Bequia United FC|Bequia United]]|clubnat=SVG|latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Tristan Marshall]]|age={{bda|2003|12|19|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Pacific FC]]|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jazzi Barnum-Bobb]]|age={{bda|1995|9|15|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Chelmsford City F.C.|Chelmsford City]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Andrew Johnson (footballer, born 2004)|Andrew Johnson]]|age={{bda|2004|2|3|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Cornell Big Red men's soccer|Cornell Big Red]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Brandon John]]|age={{bda|1995|1|5|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Master's FA]]|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Joseph Barter]]|age={{bda|1998|7|15|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Haywards Heath Town F.C.|Haywards Heath Town]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Chevron McLean]]|age={{bda|1996|5|9|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Cheshunt F.C.|Cheshunt]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mekeal Williams]]|age={{bda|2001|2|12|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Jebelle FC|Jebelle]]|clubnat=SVG|latest=v. {{fb|BON}}, 17 November 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kevin Francis (Saint Vincent footballer)|Kevin Francis]]|age={{bda|1994|1|21|df=y}}|caps=51|goals=2|club=[[System 3 FC|System 3]]|clubnat=SVG|latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 13 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Justin Robertson (footballer)|Justin Robertson]]|age={{bda|2000|6|23|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Master's FA]]|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 13 October 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kyle Edwards (footballer, born 1997)|Kyle Edwards]]|age={{bda|1997|1|15|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=4|club=[[Hartford Athletic]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Sherwyn Alexander]]|age={{bda|1997|10|12|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Jebelle FC|Jebelle]]|clubnat=SVG|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Micah Joseph]]|age={{bda|2003|5|21|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Simcoe County Rovers FC|Simcoe County Rovers]]|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[J'Quan Glasgow]]|age=|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Avenues United FC|Avenues United]]|clubnat=SVG|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Shak Adams]]|age={{bda|df=y|1998|9|7}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Unattached]]|clubnat=|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Tre Crosby]]|age={{bda|df=y|1995|1|2}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Unionville Milliken SC]]|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|SLV}}, 13 October 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Gidson Francis]]|age={{bda|1999|4|1|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}; 8 September 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Oalex Anderson]]|age={{bda|1995|11|11|df=y}}|caps=42|goals=20|club=[[North Carolina FC|North Carolina]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Oryan Velox]]|age={{bda|2004|10|28|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=3|club=[[VfR Aalen]]|clubnat=GER|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Rondell Thomas]]|age={{bda|df=y|2000|12|12}}|caps=10|goals=2|club=[[Faulkland SC|Faulkland]]|clubnat=JAM|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}; 8 September 2024}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Trezine da Souza]]|age={{bda|df=y|2000|5|11}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Jebelle FC|Jebelle]]|clubnat=SVG|latest=v. {{fb|MSR}}; 8 September 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad / standby
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Serving suspension
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|8 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Saint Vincent and the Grenadines |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/157/Saint_Vincent_Grenadines.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Saint Vincent and the Grenadines.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:STEWART-WHITECAPS.jpg|250px|right|thumb|[[Cornelius Stewart]] (right) is Saint Vincent and the Grenadines' most capped player with 79 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Cornelius Stewart]]'''
|79
|27
|2007–present
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Dorren Hamlet]]
|65
|2
|2008–2023
|-
|rowspan=2|3
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Shandel Samuel]]
|63
|32
|2001–2016
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|{{ill|Kendall Velox|fr}}
|63
|15
|1992–2008
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Roy Richards]]
|59
|1
|2008–2016
|-
|rowspan=3|6
|style="text-align: left;"|{{ill|Melvin Andrews|pt}}
|58
|0
|1996–2008
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Myron Samuel]]
|58
|23
|2008–2018
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|'''{{ill|Nazir McBurnette|es}}'''
|58
|5
|2011–present
|-
|9
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Emerald George]]
|52
|5
|2007–2016
|-
|10
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Kevin Francis (Saint Vincent footballer)|Kevin Francis]]'''
|51
|2
|2014–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Shandel Samuel]]
|32
|63
|{{#expr:33/63 round 2}}
|2001–2016
|-
|2
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Cornelius Stewart]]'''
|27
|79
|{{#expr:27/79 round 2}}
|2007–present
|-
|3
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Oalex Anderson]]'''
|24
|52
|{{#expr:24/52 round 2}}
|2014–present
|-
|4
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Rodney Jack]]
|24
|36
|{{#expr:24/36 round 2}}
|1992–2004
|-
|5
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Myron Samuel]]
|23
|58
|{{#expr:23/58 round 2}}
|2008–2018
|-
|6
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Alwyn Guy]]
|16
|39
|{{#expr:16/39 round 2}}
|1996–2008
|-
|7
|style="text-align: left;"|{{ill|Kendall Velox|fr}}
|15
|63
|{{#expr:15/63 round 2}}
|1992–2008
|-
|8
|style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Chavel Cunningham]]'''
|12
|28
|{{#expr:12/28 round 2}}
|2015–present
|-
|rowspan=2|9
|style="text-align: left;"|{{ill|Andre Hinds|fr}}
|11
|11
|{{#expr:11/11 round 2}}
|1995–1996
|-
|style="text-align: left;"|[[Renson Haynes]]
|11
|18
|{{#expr:11/18 round 2}}
|2001–2006
|-
|}

==Competitive record==

===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]]
!rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
| [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
| colspan=8|''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
| colspan=6|''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|-
| [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]] to [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
| colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
| colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=9|''Did not qualify''
| 10
| 2
| 1
| 7
| 5
| 32
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
| 10
| 2
| 2
| 6
| 17
| 33
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| 12
| 5
| 0
| 7
| 24
| 29
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| 8
| 3
| 1
| 4
| 11
| 15
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 1
| 7
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 6
| 1
| 2
| 3
| 4
| 12
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 10
| 1
| 2
| 7
| 15
| 42
|-
| {{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
| 4
| 1
| 0
| 3
| 3
| 16
|-
| {{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
| 4
| 1
| 0
| 3
| 9
| 9
|-
| {{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
| {{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/9|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!66
!16
!8
!42
!89
!195
|}

===CONCACAF Gold Cup===
{{main|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines at the CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=10|[[CONCACAF Championship]] & [[CONCACAF Gold Cup|Gold Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
|-
|{{flagicon|El Salvador}} [[1963 CONCACAF Championship|1963]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=9|''Not a CONCACAF member''
|-
|{{flagicon|Guatemala}} [[1965 CONCACAF Championship|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Honduras|1949}} [[1967 CONCACAF Championship|1967]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Costa Rica}} [[1969 CONCACAF Championship|1969]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[1971 CONCACAF Championship|1971]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Haiti|1964}} [[1973 CONCACAF Championship|1973]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1977 CONCACAF Championship|1977]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Honduras|1949}} [[1981 CONCACAF Championship|1981]]
|-
|[[1985 CONCACAF Championship|1985]]
|-
|[[1989 CONCACAF Championship|1989]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1991 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1991]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[1993 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1993]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]]
|Group stage
|9th
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|8
|[[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup squads#Saint Vincent and the Grenadines|Squad]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1998 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1998]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2000 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2000]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2002 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2003 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2003]]
|colspan=9|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2005 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2005]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=11|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2007 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2007]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2013 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2015 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2017 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2017]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Costa Rica}} {{flagicon|Jamaica}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2025]]
|-
!Total
!Group stage
!1/18
!2
!0
!0
!2
!0
!8
!–
|}
{| class="wikitable collapsible collapsed" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=2|CONCACAF Championship & Gold Cup history
|-
!First match
|{{fb|MEX}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|VIN}}<br>(11 January 1996; [[San Diego]], United States)
|-
!Biggest Win
|–
|-
!Biggest Defeat
|{{fb|MEX}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|VIN}}<br>(11 January 1996; [[San Diego]], United States)
|-
!Best Result
|Group stage in [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]]
|-
!Worst Result
|–
|-
|}

===CONCACAF Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
! colspan="20" |[[CONCACAF Nations League]] record
|-
! colspan="10" |League
! rowspan="7" |
! colspan="9" |Finals
|-
!Season
!Division
!Group
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/relegation at end of season}}
!Finals
!Result
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Squad|Finals Squad Only}}
|-
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League|2019–20]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group D|D]]
|6
|3
|2
|1
|6
|4
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2021 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2021]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="4" |''Ineligible''
|-
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group C|C]]
|6
|0
|2
|4
|5
|14
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2023 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2023]]
|-
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League|2023–24]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League#Group C|C]]
|6
|3
|0
|3
|13
|14
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2024 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2024]]
|-
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group A|A]]
|6
|4
|1
|1
|12
|7
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2025 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2025]]
|-
!Total
!—
!—
!24
!10
!5
!9
!36
!39
!—
!Total
!0 Titles
!—
!—
!—
!—
!—
!—
!—
|}

===Caribbean Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Caribbean Cup]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!Pld
!W
!D*
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|{{flagicon|BAR}} [[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]]
|Group stage || 6th || 2 || 0 || 1 || 1 || 1 || 3
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1990 Caribbean Cup|1990]]
|colspan=2|''Abandoned''<ref>Play was suspended when [[Jamaat al Muslimeen]] [[Jamaat al Muslimeen coup attempt|attempted a ''coup d'état'']] of the [[Politics of Trinidad and Tobago|government of Trinidad and Tobago]]. The tournament was abandoned altogether after [[1990 Atlantic hurricane season#Tropical Storm Arthur|Tropical storm Arthur]] forced the cancellation of the final round of games. Trinidad and Tobago were to meet Martinique in the final.</ref> ||2||0||0||2||2||5
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1991 Caribbean Cup|1991]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1992 Caribbean Cup|1992]]
|Group stage || 8th || 3 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 1 || 4
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[1993 Caribbean Cup|1993]]
|Group stage || 8th || 3 || 0 || 0 || 3 || 4 || 10
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1994 Caribbean Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|- bgcolor=#C0C0C0
|{{flagicon|CAY}} {{flagicon|JAM}} [[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995]]
|'''Runners-up''' || '''2nd''' || '''5''' || '''3''' || '''1''' || '''1''' || '''13''' || '''11'''
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1996 Caribbean Cup|1996]]
|Group stage || 7th || 3 || 0 || 1 || 2 || 2 || 7
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} {{flagicon|SKN}} [[1997 Caribbean Cup|1997]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} {{flagicon|TRI}} [[1998 Caribbean Cup|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1999 Caribbean Cup|1999]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2001 Caribbean Cup|2001]]
|-
|{{flagicon|BRB}} [[2005 Caribbean Cup|2005]]
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007]]
|Group stage || 6th || 3 || 1 || 0 || 2 || 2 || 4
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2010 Caribbean Cup|2010]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ATG}} [[2012 Caribbean Cup|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|JAM}} [[2014 Caribbean Cup|2014]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MTQ|snake}} [[2017 Caribbean Cup|2017]]
|-
!Total
!Runners-up
!7/19
!21
!4
!3
!14
!25
!44
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[CFU Championship]] / [[Caribbean Cup]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (3): [[1979 CFU Championship|1979]], [[1981 CFU Championship|1981]], [[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{commons category}}
*{{official|http://svgff.org/}} of the National team
*[https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/stvin-recintlp.html Individual Records] at the [[RSSSF]]

{{Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team}}
{{Navboxes
|title=Saint Vincent and the Grenadines squads – CONCACAF Gold Cup
 | bg              = #FCD116
 | fg              = #009E60
 | bordercolor     = #0072C6
|list1=
{{Saint Vincent and the Grenadines squad 1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
}}
{{Football in Saint Vincent and the Grenadines}}
{{CONCACAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Saint Vincent And The Grenadines National Football Team}}
[[Category:Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team| ]]
[[Category:Caribbean national association football teams]]
